blocks.md:
## "Tell me about yourself" — assuming a general strong-tech HM round (say the word and I'll re-tune for Amazon/Google/DigitalOcean)

> 💬 If they name a company, I'll bias the closer toward their values. Default here is a clean career-arc intro, ~150 words / ~75 seconds.

🎙️ **Say-it script:**

> So I'm a backend engineer, about three and a half years in, all of it on high-throughput systems. Right now I'm at Walmart Data Ventures as an SE-3, building the supplier-facing data APIs — the stuff Pepsi and Coca-Cola use to pull their sales and inventory out of Walmart. Spring Boot, Kafka, BigQuery, that kind of stack.
>
> Before this I was at a startup, Good Creator Co. I was basically the sole architect for six production services on a five-person team. The one I'm proudest of — I moved our analytics off Postgres onto ClickHouse and cut query time from 30 seconds to 12, with about a 30% infra cost drop.
>
> And I started at PayU, on loan disbursal — real customer money, my first time on-call. Got a partner API failure rate down from 4.6% to under half a percent.
>
> So the arc's been startup scrappiness to platform scale. Now I want to push on infra at a bigger scale, which is why I'm here.

💬 **One-line version:**
> Backend engineer, ~3.5 years — PayU loan systems, then sole architect of six services at a startup, now building supplier data APIs at Walmart. I want platform work at bigger scale, which is what brought me here.

**If they probe "what do you actually do day-to-day at Walmart":**
- Supplier-facing APIs on Spring Boot 3 / Java 17, Kafka and Kafka Connect into BigQuery.
- Built a reusable audit-logging library adopted by 12+ teams; killed a ~$50K/mo tool down to ~$500/mo.
- Led the Spring Boot 2→3 migration with zero downtime, and mentored a junior to promotion.

**What NOT to say:**
- Don't list every one of the six services — name the ClickHouse one and stop.
- Don't bad-mouth Walmart when you hit the "why leaving" beat — keep it "bigger scale," not "running from."
- Don't drift into deep tech in the intro; save the depth for when they ask.

Want me to retune this for a specific company (Amazon LP-flavored, Google, or DigitalOcean's 7 values)?
